[2023-03-18 07:28] silentslayers: I think a league would be great for a couple of reasons: 

- More relaxed than a tournament
 
- A good fit for something with a loose schedule (e.g. You can take like a whole month to play your games and it’s np)

- Isn’t just a one time deal, as in, it doesn’t just end like a tournament does. It will give recurring entertainment value. 

- Streaming is also not time sensitive, like if the streamers/commentators are busy on a certain week, that’s fine because the league is not something time sensitive.

- More inclusive: if people want to join later after it starts, we can still squeeze them in.

One of the main barriers seems to be additional organizational overhead. I think we should try to make it as simple as possible, like just making a simple spreadsheet that tracks everyone’s stats with a replay beside it. Maybe we can even trust people to fill in the spreadsheet themselves. It would be easy to spot if anything gets inaccurate. We can have a back up sheet in case anybody griefs it by accident.

Also probably easier for something that multiple people can organize compared to a tournament where you’re more relying on the main tournament organizer. Overall probably more autonomous too.
